name: onewire
description: Dallas 1-Wire bus protocol driver. Used with DS18B20, DS18S20 temperature sensors and other OneWire devices. Built into MicroPython firmware.
differences_from_cpython: This module does NOT exist in CPython. It is MicroPython-specific for embedded 1-Wire bus communication.

classes.name: OneWire

constructor_params.name: pin
constructor_params.description: Data pin. Must support open-drain output and input. Any GPIO.

methods.name: OneWire.reset

returns.description: True if any device is present on the bus, False otherwise.

methods.description: Send reset pulse and detect presence pulse. Must be called first before communicating.

methods.name: OneWire.readbit
methods.description: Read a single bit from the bus.

methods.name: OneWire.readbyte
methods.description: Read a byte from the bus.

methods.name: OneWire.readinto
methods.description: Read bytes from the bus into the provided buffer.

methods.name: OneWire.writebit
methods.description: Write a single bit to the bus.

methods.name: OneWire.writebyte
methods.description: Write a byte to the bus.

methods.name: OneWire.crc8
methods.description: Compute Dallas 1-Wire CRC-8 checksum. Useful for validating ROM addresses.

methods.name: OneWire.select_rom

parameters.name: rom
parameters.description: 8-byte ROM address of the device to select.

methods.description: Select a specific device by its ROM address. Must call reset() first.

methods.name: OneWire.scan

returns.description: List of 8-byte ROM addresses for all devices on the bus.

methods.description: Scan the 1-Wire bus and return ROM addresses of all devices found.

classes.example:
from machine import Pin
import onewire

ow = onewire.OneWire(Pin(4))
roms = ow.scan()
print('Found devices:', len(roms))
for rom in roms:
    print('ROM:', rom.hex())

# Select a device and communicate
ow.reset()
ow.select_rom(roms[0])
ow.writebyte(0xBE)  # read scratchpad
buf = bytearray(9)
ow.readinto(buf)
print('Data:', buf)

wiring: DATA -> Any GPIO pin (connect 4.7kohm pull-up resistor to 3.3V)
VCC -> 3.3V (or parasitic power mode: VCC -> GND)
Pull-up resistor is REQUIRED - the 1-Wire bus is open-drain.
esp32_notes: Works on any GPIO pin (bit-banged, not hardware-dependent).
1-Wire protocol is timing-critical. Disable interrupts during communication if possible.
For DS18B20, use the ds18x20 module (install via mip) which builds on onewire for convenience.
Long wires (>1m) may need a stronger pull-up (2.2kohm) or active pull-up.
